title: hypericaceae
intro: The Hypericaceae rank, also known as the St. John's Wort family, is a taxonomic group of flowering plants that are typically shrubs or small trees with five-petaled flowers. This family is native to the Northern Hemisphere, with a majority of its species found in Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The Hypericaceae family comprises around 40-50 species, which are often known for their yellow, orange, or red flowers. Many species within this family have been used in traditional medicine for their supposed antidepressant properties.
